subject: Statistics
title: The Distribution of Everything
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: The Distribution of Everything.

hook.text: Stars, neurons, river lengths, and stock returns all follow known distribution patterns.

buildup.text: Nature doesn't pick random shapes. Physics and economics create predictable distributional forms.

discovery.text: Additive processes create normal distributions. Multiplicative ones create log-normal or power laws.

twist.text: Knowing which process generates your data tells you which distribution to expect — and fear.

climax.text: Distributions are the grammar of randomness. Learn them and you can read any data.

punchline.text: Every pattern of randomness has a name. Learn them.

quiz.question: What determines which distribution data follows?
options: The process that generates it — additive yields normal, multiplicative yields power laws
Random chance — any distribution can appear from any process
